title: PTD Today — Media
disclaimer: Informational only — AI-generated summaries; may contain errors. Verify with the original video.

items.title: Would you go to an in-person dating app event?

ai.summary: Based on the available description, dating app Hinge announced a $1 million fund to support social groups in New York, Los Angeles and London, underwriting free or affordable social events for young people. The move is a global expansion of Hinge’s One More Hour social impact initiative, positioned as a response to rising loneliness among Gen Z.
bullets: Hinge launched a $1 million fund for social groups.
Funding targets New York, Los Angeles and London.
Grants are intended to enable free or low-cost social events for young people.
This expands Hinge’s existing One More Hour social impact program.
Framed as addressing increasing loneliness among Gen Z.
takeaways: Track localized initiatives in major metros (NYC, LA, London) for potential short-term demand tied to sponsored events.
Evaluate readiness of event-related infrastructure (connectivity, on-site networking) in venues you serve or partner with.
Consider outreach or partnership opportunities with app-driven community programs for venue or tech support.
Monitor similar corporate social programs as indicators of localized, event-driven service needs.

items.title: Build Knowledge Graph-powered AI Agents For Your Use Case | Databases for AI

ai.summary: Based on the available description… This session demonstrates building a knowledge graph automatically from raw documents and connecting it to an AI agent so the agent can understand your company's domain. The presenters show an end-to-end build that requires no prior graph expertise.
bullets: Knowledge graphs address AI agents' lack of company-specific knowledge.
Build a knowledge graph automatically from raw documents.
Connect the graph to an agent that understands your business domain.
No graph queries or graph theory expertise required.
Includes an end-to-end demo; references a prior episode comparing VectorDB vs GraphDB.
takeaways: KG-powered agents can let AI access and reason over proprietary operational documents for domain-specific questions.
Automated graph construction reduces the need to train teams in graph theory or complex query languages.
An end-to-end approach can speed pilots for applying AI agents to operational workflows and decision support.
Easier integration of diverse documents into a structured knowledge layer enables more reliable, business-aware AI responses.

items.title: Leveraging tax credits and incentives for sustainability projects

ai.summary: Based on the available description, Randa Barsoum of PwC US explains that energy incentives are only part of the tax picture for sustainability projects and highlights other, less obvious tax credits and incentives. She calls out state and local abatements, location-based incentives, and R&D tax credits tied to innovation (for example, reimagining packaging or processes), and emphasizes that a broader view of sustainability investments can unlock meaningful tax value.
bullets: Energy incentives are common but not the only tax levers for sustainability.
State and local abatements can support sustainability initiatives.
Location-based incentives are another underused tool.
R&D tax credits can apply to innovation efforts like reimagining packaging or processes.
Viewing sustainability investments through a broader lens can unlock meaningful tax value.
takeaways: Factor state and local abatements and location-based incentives into site selection and tax planning.
Evaluate R&D tax credits for innovation in processes or operations related to sustainability efforts.
Incorporate broader tax-incentive opportunities (beyond energy credits) into sustainability business cases and financial models.

ai.summary: Based on the available description, the video uses starry imagery to show Copilot helping Christa "find the star within." It closes with a clear call to action to download and try Copilot via the link in the bio.
bullets: Description evokes a sky full of stars.
States Copilot helps Christa "find the star within."
Uses a star emoji (🌟) for emphasis.
Includes a direct call to action: "Download & try Copilot at link in bio."
Short, evocative promotional message.
takeaways: Messaging emphasizes personal empowerment—expect user-focused positioning when evaluating Copilot for staff.

Visual, emotive branding signals consumer-friendly outreach—anticipate adoption support needs focused on discoverability.

items.title: Bringing In $251K A Year With 3 Jobs In The Bay Area

ai.summary: Based on the available description, Naseema McElroy, 44, is a labor and delivery nurse in Brentwood, California who also runs a personal finance brand called Financially Intentional. After accruing nearly $1 million in debt in 2015, she documented her debt-payoff journey, became debt-free by 2017, and brings in $251K a year working three jobs in the Bay Area.
bullets: Naseema McElroy — 44, labor and delivery nurse and founder of Financially Intentional.
Accumulated nearly $1 million in debt in 2015.
Started her business to document and share her debt-payoff journey.
Was debt-free by 2017.
Reports $251K annual income from three jobs in the Bay Area.
takeaways: Diversified income sources can materially increase annual revenue — consider multiple service lines or contracts to stabilize revenue.
Public documentation of financial strategy can build a brand and trust with stakeholders — transparent reporting may support business development.
Aggressive debt-reduction strategies can be effective over a concentrated timeframe — evaluate accelerated paydown options for high-cost liabilities.

items.title: GeForce Garage - Summer of RTX Community Build

ai.summary: Based on the available description, this GeForce Garage video announces the winner of the Summer of RTX r/buildapc Community Build contest, u/JeffersonPutnam, who built a small-form-factor GeForce RTX 5090 system. The description provides the full parts list and specs (chassis, GPU, CPU, RAM, power, and AIO) and links to the PCPartPicker build and GeForce social channels.
bullets: Winner: u/JeffersonPutnam’s SFF GeForce RTX 5090 build for the Summer of RTX community contest.
Host: GeForce host Dwight (Twitter: @_MightyPotato).
Key components listed: Fractal Design Era 2 chassis, GeForce RTX 5090 Founders Edition, Ryzen 7 9800X3D, MSI MPG B850I Edge.
Supporting hardware: 32GB DDR5-6000 RAM, Corsair SF1000 power supply, Corsair Nautilus 240 RS AIO.
PCPartPicker link provided for the complete winning system specs.
takeaways: The build demonstrates the GeForce RTX 5090 Founders Edition deployed in a small-form-factor system alongside specific power and cooling choices (Corsair SF1000, Nautilus 240 RS), offering concrete component pairings to review.
A compact chassis (Fractal Design Era 2) is shown accommodating a flagship GPU and high-performance CPU, useful as a reference for space-constrained deployments or edge use cases.
The PCPartPicker parts list link provides a ready reference for compatibility and BOM details that can inform procurement and integration assessments.

items.title: TTEC accelerates AI deployment and revenue streams with Google Cloud Consulting

ai.summary: Based on the available description… TTEC partnered with Google Cloud Consulting to rapidly prototype and deploy AI solutions, enabling new revenue streams. They used GenAI and Google Cloud infrastructure to streamline operations, boost developer productivity, and compress their digital transformation timeline.
bullets: TTEC worked with Google Cloud Consulting to accelerate AI prototyping and deployment.
Generative AI and Google Cloud infrastructure were core to the initiative.
The program unlocked entirely new revenue streams for TTEC.
Efforts focused on operational streamlining and higher developer productivity.
Google Cloud Consulting helped compress the company’s digital transformation timeline.
takeaways: Ensure cloud infrastructure can support GenAI workloads to enable new revenue opportunities.
Engage cloud consulting to shorten prototyping-to-production timelines and reduce internal burden.
Improved developer productivity accelerates time-to-market for AI-driven services.
Operational efficiency gains from AI can directly impact cost and service delivery.
Partnering with cloud experts can expand what’s feasible for business transformation.
